title: podman rmi --ignore
body:
Add an `--ignore` flag to `podman image rm` to instruct ignoring image
if a specified image does not exist and to not throw an error.  Other
commands (e.g., `podman container rm`) already support this flag.

Such an `--ignore` flag can come in handy in clean-up scripcts such as
the teardown phases in the Podman tests.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@redhat.com>

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: Ah ... a regression from the apparmor change.

> Ah ... a regression from the apparmor change.

https://github.com/containers/common/pull/969

Looks like the `auth.go` changes broke login.

This seems like an uncomfortably huge set of changes just for `rmi --ignore`. I wonder if it might be better to break it down into smaller changes, such as a clean revendor first?
It's curious that the tests don't break on Fedora oO

> It's curious that the tests don't break on Fedora oO

Ah, just had to refresh the page.
I'll open a new vendor PR with changes to the tests.

https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13554 is fixing the login tests.

However, I see one networking test fail as well:
> [+1995s] [Fail] Podman network create [It] podman network create with multiple subnets dual stack with gateway and range 
[+1995s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/network_create_test.go:424

@Luap99 do you know what's going on there?

> #13554 is fixing the login tests.
> 
> However, I see one networking test fail as well:
> 
> > [+1995s] [Fail] Podman network create [It] podman network create with multiple subnets dual stack with gateway and range
> > [+1995s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/network_create_test.go:424
> 
> @Luap99 do you know what's going on there?

Yes I see the problem, I will open a PR to fix it.

LGTM
once the tests are happy.
